reference: Hi, as you narrated, head trauma caused by a heavy object or protruding part may lead to a localized depressed fracture of the skull bone, and such a defect may not produce many symptoms. This type of defect may only be present as a dent upon palpation. However, such a fracture usually does not require any treatment or surgery. If you are very concerned, then you may undergo a plain computed tomography scan of the head with a bone window to look for any fracture. I hope you find my advice useful. Wishing you good health.
text: Hi, as you're already having a hard fall, having on a red foot, bruised part may lead to a localized fracture of the shoulder. And such a defect may not be seen at first. This type of defect may only be present at the site of bone calcification. However, such a fracture usually does not require any special treatment. If you are very concerned, then you may undergo a plain computed tomography scan of the head with a bone window to look for any fracture. I hope you find my advice useful. Wishing you good health.
